resolutionSummary: Phase 4 implements the required blockerClass finding classification on the active verdict and verifier-report schemas, wires located gate validation into reviewer_output, adds producer prompt guidance, and covers the behavior with targeted schema and gate tests. The review-fixes exclusion is sound because that schema has no finding item or severity field to classify.

riskDisposition.risk: specs/review-adaptive-routing-cost-control/tasks.md was marked unclassified despite parent inventory classification
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: The path is an untracked design artifact explicitly listed as unrelated to the phase commit. I inspected the actual tasks file for P4 requirements and used it only as a design source.

riskDisposition.risk: Source Manifest used brace shorthand for specs/review-adaptive-routing-cost-control/{spec,plan,tasks}.md
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: The shorthand is a process-evidence issue in the review package, not an implementation defect. I resolved and inspected the exact files, so no review scope was lost.

riskDisposition.risk: Source Manifest used wildcard shorthand for packages/core/agenthub/skills/3rd-review/__fixtures__/*.json
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: I expanded and inspected the five exact fixture files. They match the intended legal, illegal, missing, output_contract, and process_evidence cases.

riskDisposition.risk: Source Manifest used TASK_DIR-relative shorthand for artifacts/decision-log.md
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: I inspected the exact task decision log path and verified D6 plus D6-apply-1 context.

riskDisposition.risk: Skill path declarations include placeholder and home-relative shorthand paths
riskDisposition.checkedSource: packages/core/agenthub/skills/3rd-review/SKILL.md lines around required skill fallback; code-reviewer contract
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: The <name> placeholders are generic fallback instructions for non-code reviews. This checkpoint is code-review, and the code-review contract does not require executing those non-code skills.
The code-reviewer contract has no required external skill execution section. The SKILL.md required-skill execution rule applies to non-code reviews only.

riskDisposition.risk: scope-inventory needed to rule out forbidden or out-of-scope edits
riskDisposition.checkedSource: git status --short; git show --name-only 4dc45a0ae^..4dc45a0ae; root and package CLAUDE files
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: Committed Phase 4 files are confined to agenthub schema, gate, tests, fixtures, and SKILL.md producer wiring. No forbidden core files or product UI/backend paths were modified.

riskDisposition.risk: Mechanical grep coverage was missing for changed ts/md files
riskDisposition.checkedSource: rg over changed ts/md files; node scripts/provider-name-scan.mjs; git diff --check
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: Targeted grep found only pre-existing allowed technical/test references. provider-name-scan passed over the managed prompt set, and git diff --check passed.

riskDisposition.risk: worktree hygiene has unrelated dirty paths
riskDisposition.whyNotBlocking: Dirty paths are packages/core/.machine, packages/core/agenthub/host/__test_intake_bg__, and specs/review-adaptive-routing-cost-control. The first two are transient test state and the specs directory is an untracked design artifact; Phase 4 implementation is committed in HEAD.

facts: review-slice maps decision D6 to FR-CLASS machine enforcement via schema prevention.
review-slice maps missing blockerClass intent to FR-CLASS-001: every finding must carry blockerClass and missing fails fast.
review-slice maps process_evidence blocking concern to FR-CLASS-002: process_evidence plus blocking is forbidden, while important/minor remains legal.
review-slice maps count/severity substitution concern to FR-CLASS-003: blockerClass is a dedicated classification field.
review-slice maps override concern to FR-CLASS-004 explicit non-goal: no new human-advance-over-revise override entry.
review-slice explicitly excludes packages/core/agenthub/host/__test_intake_bg__/ as unrelated transient intake background dirs.
